issues.message: Image is missing useful alt text

evidence.reason: Image is missing useful alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add alt text that describes the image purpose, or mark decorative images as presentation.

rule.title: Image missing useful alt text
rule.why_it_matters: Students using screen readers miss the image content entirely, which can hide instructions, diagrams, or required information.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the image is informative or decorative; only a human can judge if existing alt text is actually useful.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static checks cannot judge alt text quality, only whether it exists.

issues.message: Form control is missing an accessible label

evidence.snippet: <textarea id="g-recaptcha-response-100000" name="g-recaptcha-response" class="g-recaptcha-response" style="width: 250px; height: 40px; border: 1px solid rgb(193, 193, 193); margin: 10px 25px; paddi...
evidence.reason: Form control has no accessible label.
evidence.name: g-recaptcha-response

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible <label> connected with for/id. Use aria-label only when a visible label is not possible.

rule.title: Form control missing accessible label
rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the visual design already implies the label but fails programmatically.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot always confirm labels created dynamically by JavaScript.

issues.message: Rendered text may have low contrast

evidence.reason: Computed foreground/background contrast is below the conservative 4.5:1 review threshold.

issues.suggested_fix: Increase foreground/background contrast and confirm the final design with manual review.

rule.title: Rendered text may have low contrast
rule.why_it_matters: Low-vision readers may not be able to read text when the computed foreground and background colors are too similar.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm the sampled colors match what users actually see, especially over gradients, images, and layered components.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check samples browser-computed colors and does not prove full WCAG color contrast compliance.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.text: We value your privacy We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve
evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Low-vision browser checks are conservative heuristics and require manual review.

limitations: Browser mode approximates keyboard interaction but does not simulate a full screen reader.
Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
Focus overlay shows observed Tab stops in this browser run.
It does not represent every assistive technology experience.

wide_elements.text: We value your privacy We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve
We value your privacy We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve
We value your privacy We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve
We use cookies to enhance your browsing experience, serve personalized ads or co

limitations: Low-vision checks use browser-computed styles and conservative heuristics.
Rendered color contrast checks do not prove full WCAG conformance.
Zoom/reflow uses a narrow viewport approximation rather than a full zoom compliance test.
Focus indicator detection may miss custom focus styles.

ai_scout.summary: Potential accessibility barriers were identified on the CAST website.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some images may lack alternative text, which could hinder users who rely on screen readers or have visual impairments.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users who rely on assistive technologies may not be able to understand the content of images without alternative text.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of images with empty or missing alt attributes were detected.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: A form control may be missing an accessible label, potentially causing issues for users who rely on screen readers or have visual impairments.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users who rely on assistive technologies may not be able to understand the purpose of the form control without an accessible label.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: A textarea element was detected with no accessible label.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Some text elements may have low contrast with their backgrounds, which could cause readability issues for users with visual impairments.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Users with visual impairments may have difficulty reading text with low contrast, potentially leading to frustration or abandonment of the website.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of text elements with low contrast were detected using conservative heuristics.

outreach_notes: We suggest reviewing the website's accessibility to ensure that all users can navigate and understand the content effectively.
limitations: AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review.
AI Scout output is not a confirmed accessibility finding.
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior.
AI Scout output is not a needs human review accessibility finding.
